questions.text: Depending upon the nature of the ordering, liquid crystals are divided into types.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Option (a) "More than 1" is a vague answer. While it is true that there is more than one type of liquid crystal based on the nature of the order, this option does not provide specific information about the different types.
Option (b) "Two" is not the correct answer. Liquid crystals are divided into more than two types based on the nature of ordering.

Liquid crystals can be classified into three main categories: thermotropic liquid crystals, lyotropic liquid crystals, and metallotropic liquid crystals. Thus, option C is •Thermotropic liquid crystals (LC) are anisotropic liquids that possess a mesophase (a phase with crystal and liquid properties) within a certain temperature range.•Lyotropic liquid crystals result when fat-loving and water-loving chemical compounds known as amphiphiles dissolve into a solution that behaves both like a liquid and a solid crystal.•The addition of long-chain soap-like molecules leads to a series of new phases that show a variety of liquid crystalline behavior both as a function of the inorganic-organic composition ratio and of temperature. This class of materials has been named metallotropic.
Option (d) "Four" is not the correct answer. Liquid crystals are divided into more than four types based on the nature of ordering.

questions.explanation: Liquid crystals can be classified into three main categories: thermotropic liquid crystals, lyotropic liquid crystals, and metallotropic liquid crystals. Thus, option C is •Thermotropic liquid crystals (LC) are anisotropic liquids that possess a mesophase (a phase with crystal and liquid properties) within a certain temperature range.•Lyotropic liquid crystals result when fat-loving and water-loving chemical compounds known as amphiphiles dissolve into a solution that behaves both like a liquid and a solid crystal.•The addition of long-chain soap-like molecules leads to a series of new phases that show a variety of liquid crystalline behavior both as a function of the inorganic-organic composition ratio and of temperature. This class of materials has been named metallotropic.

questions.text: A liquid crystalline state exists between two temperatures i.e melting temperature and _ temperature.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The boiling temperature is generally not associated with the transition between liquid crystal and isotropic liquid states. The clearing temperature is more relevant in this context.

Freezing is the transition from a liquid to a solid state, and it is not directly related to the transition involving liquid crystals. The clearing temperature is the relevant point for this transition.

In the context of liquid crystals, the transition between the ordered liquid crystalline state and the isotropic liquid state is often referred to as the "clearing" temperature. So, a liquid crystalline state exists between the melting temperature and the clearing temperature.

While melting temperature is mentioned in the question, boiling and freezing temperatures are not directly associated with the transition between liquid crystal and isotropic liquid states. Therefore, "all of these" is not the correct answer.

questions.explanation: So, a liquid crystalline state exists between two temperatures i.e. melting temperature and clearing temperature. The property of liquid crystals is intermediate between those of crystals and isotropic liquids. That is why they are called liquid crystals or mesomorphic states.

questions.text: The properties of liquid crystals are intermediate between crystals and:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Anisotropic liquids are liquids that exhibit different physical properties in different directions in space. This is because of the random arrangement of particles. Hence, this option is the incorrect one.

The nematic liquid is a type of anisotropic liquid that lacks positional order; its atoms are randomly arranged. They have elongated molecules, but not all liquid crystals are nematic. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Correct. Isotropic liquids have no long-range order and uniform properties in all directions. Liquid crystals have more order than isotropic liquids but less than solid crystals, making them intermediate between crystals and isotropic liquids.
Incorrect. This is too general. The proper comparison is to isotropic liquids, which lack directional order, unlike liquid crystals.

questions.explanation: Correct. Isotropic liquids have no long-range order and uniform properties in all directions. Liquid crystals have more order than isotropic liquids but less than solid crystals, making them intermediate between crystals and isotropic liquids.

questions.text: Which of the following has more evaporation rate at the same temperature?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: A higher evaporation rate ( higher volatility) means that intermolecular forces are weaker and hence are easily overcome leading to the molecules escaping from the surface of the liquid. Gasoline is a mixture of volatile, flammable hydrocarbons. Hydrocarbons have weak instantaneous dipole-induced dipole forces between them. Water and Alcohol both have strong intermolecular forces: hydrogen bonding, which requires more energy to overcome. Honey being very viscous/ having higher density has the strongest intermolecular forces of attraction ( greater number of hydrogen bonds).

Water has stronger intermolecular forces due to hydrogen bonding, and its molecular weight is higher compared to gasoline. While water can evaporate, it tends to evaporate more slowly than substances with weaker intermolecular forces.
Honey is a highly viscous liquid with strong intermolecular forces, and its evaporation rate is generally much slower than that of liquids with weaker forces.
Ethanol has a lower molecular weight compared to water and has weaker intermolecular forces. Therefore, ethanol is likely to have a higher evaporation rate than water.

questions.explanation: A higher evaporation rate ( higher volatility) means that intermolecular forces are weaker and hence are easily overcome leading to the molecules escaping from the surface of the liquid. questions.text: The amount of heat required to form vapors of one mole of a liquid at its boiling point is called?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since the molar heat of fusion is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the solid to the heat of sublimation is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the liquid to the gaseous state at its sublimation liquid state at its melting point.

Option B is correct since the change from liquid to vapors at its boiling point is called evaporation or vaporization. Since the change is for one mole, we call it the molar heat of evaporation/vaporization.
Option C is incorrect since molar heat of sublimation is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the liquid to the gaseous state at its sublimation point.

Option D is incorrect since molar heat of evaporation is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the liquid to the gaseous state at the surface of a boiling liquid. Sublimation is the process in which a liquid or solid changes to a gas. Evaporation is when particles escape the surface of a boiling liquid and enter a gas state.

questions.explanation: Option A is incorrect since the molar heat of fusion is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the solid to the liquid state at its melting point. Option B is correct since the change from liquid to vapors at its boiling point is called evaporation or vaporization. Since the change is for one mole, we call it the molar heat of evaporation/vaporization. Option D is incorrect since molar heat of evaporation is the amount of heat required to convert one mole of a substance from the liquid to the gaseous state at the surface of a boiling liquid. questions.text: Evaporation is the reverse of _?

questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Sublimation is the conversion of solid to gas.
Freezing is the conversion of liquid to solid.
Melting is the conversion of solid to liquid.
Evaporation is the process of a liquid changing into a gas phase. It occurs at the surface of the liquid. On the other hand, condensation is the reverse process in which a gas changes into a liquid.

questions.explanation: Evaporation is the process of a liquid changing into a gas phase. It occurs at the surface of the liquid. On the other hand, condensation is the reverse process in which a gas changes into a liquid.

options.explanation: This term is not directly related to evaporation.

Evaporation is a process of converting a liquid into its gaseous state. Exothermic: This term is not related to evaporation as it refers to a process that releases heat.
Evaporation can occur spontaneously under certain conditions such as high temperature, low pressure, and high surface area.

questions.explanation: The molecules are absorbing heat, evaporation is called endothermic. Remember, endo means “to enter” while thermic means “heat.”So automatically, you choose the opposite of endothermic i.e. Exothermic.

questions.text: Vapor pressure is measured by calculating the difference in liquid pressure and?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Mercury pressure is incorrect because it does not have any direct relation to the measurement of vapor pressure.
Glass pressure is incorrect because it does not have any direct relation to the measurement of vapor pressure.

Vapor pressure or equilibrium vapor pressure is defined as the pressure exerted by a vapor in thermodynamic equilibrium with its condensed phases at a given temperature in a closed system. The equilibrium vapor pressure is an indication of a liquid's evaporation rate.

Container pressure is also incorrect because the pressure exerted by the vapor in the container is a part of the container pressure itself. To obtain the vapor pressure, we need to subtract the atmospheric pressure from the total pressure exerted by the mixture of vapor and air in the container.

The equilibrium vapor pressure is an indication of a liquid's evaporation rate.Option D) Container pressure is also incorrect because the pressure exerted by the vapor in the container is a part of the container pressure itself. questions.text: The amount of heat required to vaporize one mole of liquid at its _ is called molar heat of vaporization.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The melting point is the temperature at which a substance changes from its solid state to its liquid state. It's the temperature at which the particles in a substance gain enough energy to overcome the forces holding them in a rigid structure and transition into a fluid state.Just like the freezing point, different substances have different melting points. For example, the melting point of ice (solid water) is 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit), which means it will start to melt and turn into liquid water at that temperature.The melting point is an important characteristic of substances and is used in various fields, such as determining the purity of a substance or understanding its behavior under different conditions.

The molar heat of vaporization, also known as the enthalpy of vaporization, is the amount of heat required to vaporize one mole of a substance at its boiling point while keeping the pressure constant.The molar heat of vaporization can be calculated using the equation:ΔHvap = q/m

The freezing point is the temperature at which a substance changes from its liquid state to its solid state. It's the temperature at which the particles in a substance slow down enough to form a solid structure.Different substances have different freezing points. For example, the freezing point of water is 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit), while the freezing point of ethanol is -114 degrees Celsius (-173 degrees Fahrenheit).The freezing point is an important property of substances and is used in various applications, such as determining the freezing point of a solution or controlling the freezing process in industries like food preservation.

Incorrect option!The molar heat of vaporization, also known as the enthalpy of vaporization, is the amount of heat required to vaporize one mole of a substance at its boiling point while keeping the pressure constant.The molar heat of vaporization can be calculated using the equation:ΔHvap = q/m.

questions.explanation: Boiling point is the phenomenon in which one mole of a liquid is vaporized.The molar heat of vaporization, also known as the enthalpy of vaporization, is the amount of heat required to vaporize one mole of a substance at its boiling point while keeping the pressure constant.The molar heat of vaporization can be calculated using the equation:ΔHvap = q/m
hints: amount of heat required to vaporize

questions.text: The temperature at which the vapor pressure of a liquid becomes equal to external pressure or atmospheric pressure is called as?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The melting point is the temperature at which the solid phase of a substance changes to the liquid phase.

The temperature at which the vapor pressure of a liquid becomes equal to the external pressure or atmospheric pressure is called the boiling point of the liquid. The boiling point is the temperature at which the molecules of the liquid have enough energy to escape from the liquid and enter the gas phase.
The freezing point is the temperature at which the liquid phase of a substance changes to the solid phase.
The sublimation point is the temperature at which a solid substance changes directly to the gas phase without going through the liquid phase.

questions.explanation: The boiling point is the temperature at which the vapor pressure of a liquid becomes equal to the external pressure or atmospheric pressure surrounding the liquid. At this temperature, the liquid undergoes a phase change and starts to vaporize rapidly, forming bubbles within the liquid.When the vapor pressure of the liquid is lower than the external pressure, the liquid tends to evaporate slowly. As the temperature increases, the vapor pressure also increases. Eventually, at the boiling point, the vapor pressure becomes equal to the external pressure, allowing the liquid to boil and convert into a gas.

questions.text: The boiling point of water is higher than that of HF because of _ hydrogen bonding.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Both H2O and HF have electronegative oxygen and fluorine atoms respectively in their molecule. In fact, Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, so technically, HF should engage in more hydrogen bonding than water. But the scenario is different. Each water molecule has two hydrogen atoms. Whereas, each HF molecule has only one hydrogen atom. And hence, water molecule takes part in extensive and more hydrogen bonding than Hydrofluoric acid. Water molecule through its extensive hydrogen bonding forms bulky molecule and it is very difficult to break its bonds. A large amount of energy is required to break all its bonds. Thus H20 has a higher boiling point than HF.

False!Both H2O and HF have electronegative oxygen and fluorine atoms respectively in their molecule. In fact, Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, so technically, HF should engage in more hydrogen bonding than water. But the scenario is different. Each water molecule has two hydrogen atoms. Thus H20 has a higher boiling point than HF.

Thus H20 has a higher boiling point than HF.

Thus H20 has a higher boiling point than HF.

questions.text: On which factors the vapour pressure of a substance does not depend?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The physical state of matter does influence vapor pressure. For example, at a given temperature, the vapor pressure of a substance in the liquid phase will generally be higher than in the solid phase.

A liquid's vapour pressure is directly related to the intermolecular forces present between its molecules. The stronger these forces, the lower the rate of evaporation and the lower the vapour pressure.
The vapor pressure of a substance does not depend on the surface area as the surface area of a solid or liquid in contact with the gas does not affect vapor pressure.

As the temperature of a liquid or solid increases its vapor pressure also increases. Conversely, vapor pressure decreases as the temperature decreases. The vapor pressure of a liquid can be measured in a variety of ways.

questions.explanation: Option B: A liquid's vapor pressure is directly related to the intermolecular forces present between its molecules. The stronger these forces, the lower the rate of evaporation and the lower the vapor pressure. Option: C The vapor pressure of a substance does not depend on the surface area as the surface area of a solid or liquid in contact with the gas does not affect vapor pressure. Option D: As the temperature of a liquid or solid increases its vapor pressure also increases. Conversely, vapor pressure decreases as the temperature decreases. The vapor pressure of a liquid can be measured in a variety of ways.

Boiling point of halogens Decrease along the period.

Option (d) "Shows abnormal behavior" is not the correct answer. The boiling point trend of halogens down the group is regular and follows the expected pattern of decreasing boiling points.

questions.text: Melting Points of Non-Polar Molecular solids are
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The melting point of nonpolar molecular solids is generally low because the forces holding these molecules together are weak. As the temperature increases, it requires less energy to overcome these weak forces, leading to a transition from a solid to a liquid state.
Non polar molecular solids have the weakest form of intermolecular forces – instantaneous dipole-induced dipole forces – which require less energy to overcome hence they have low melting points.

The term "sharp" is not typically used to describe the melting point of a substance. The melting point is a specific temperature at which a substance changes from a solid to a liquid. It may be a distinct value or a range, but the term "sharp" is not commonly associated with this property.
This option is incorrect because the melting point of nonpolar molecular solids is indeed low, making option B the correct choice based on the typical behavior of such substances.

questions.explanation: Non-polar molecular solids have the weakest form of intermolecular forces – instantaneous dipole-induced dipole forces – which require less energy to overcome hence they have low melting points.

questions.text: Boiling Points of Polar Molecular solids are
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because polar molecular solids typically have higher boiling points due to the stronger intermolecular forces (dipole-dipole interactions or hydrogen bonding) compared to nonpolar molecular solids.

While "moderate" is a relative term, it is generally less accurate for polar molecular solids. These substances often exhibit higher boiling points compared to nonpolar molecular solids, and the term "moderate" might not fully capture the strength of the intermolecular forces involved.

Polar molecules have permanent dipoles resulting in strong intermolecular forces of attraction- permanent dipole-permanent dipole forces- which require a lot of energy to be overcome hence the boiling points are high as well.

This option is incorrect because, in general, the boiling points of polar molecular solids can be predicted based on the nature of their intermolecular forces. Polar molecules tend to have higher boiling points due to stronger intermolecular attractions.

questions.explanation: Polar molecules have permanent dipoles resulting in strong intermolecular forces of attraction- permanent dipole-permanent dipole forces- which require a lot of energy to be overcome hence the boiling points are high as well.

questions.text: The intermolecular forces bring the molecules close together and give them particular
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Intermolecular forces are related to the attractions between molecules and do not involve changes in chemical composition. Chemical properties, on the other hand, are associated with the interactions and reactions that occur at the molecular level, leading to changes in chemical composition.

The strength of the intermolecular forces determine the physical properties of the substance such as their state at room temperature, their boiling and melting points. Chemical properties are determined by the intramolecular forces i.e, the forces that exist within a molecule.

Intermolecular forces deal with the interactions between molecules rather than the composition of individual molecules. Composition refers to the types and numbers of atoms in a molecule, which is more related to chemical properties.

While intermolecular forces do influence the physical nature of substances, the term "nature" is broad and can encompass both physical and chemical aspects. However, in the context of the given question, the primary focus is on physical properties, such as melting point, boiling point, and state of matter.

questions.explanation: The strength of the intermolecular forces determines the physical properties of the substance such as their state at room temperature, their boiling and melting points. Chemical properties are determined by the intramolecular forces i.e., the forces that exist within a molecule.

questions.text: When a liquid is heated in a closed container, equilibrium is established between _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option doesn't accurately describe the process of heating a liquid in a closed container. The equilibrium established in this context primarily involves the liquid and its vapor phase, not the solid phase.

Vapur pressure is the pressure exerted by the vapors in the container when both vapors and the liquid molecules are in an equilibrium state. When a liquid is taken in a closed container and heated, so to establish equilibrium, the rate of vapourization and the rate of condensation become equal, reaching the state of equilibrium. Since no solid particles are present in such a case, all other options except ‘B’ will be considered wrong.

While there are cases where a substance can exist in multiple phases simultaneously (e.g., at the triple point), in the context of heating a liquid in a closed container, the primary equilibrium is between the liquid and vapor phases. The involvement of a solid phase is not typically observed under normal conditions.

Similar to option C, the simultaneous presence of liquid, vapor, and solid phases during the heating of a liquid in a closed container is not a typical scenario under normal conditions. The primary equilibrium is between the liquid and vapor phases.

questions.explanation: Vapur pressure is the pressure exerted by the vapors in the container when both vapors and the liquid molecules are in an equilibrium state. When a liquid is taken in a closed container and heated, so to establish equilibrium, the rate of vapourization and the rate of condensation become equal, reaching the state of equilibrium. Since no solid particles are present in such a case, all other options except ‘B’ will be considered wrong.

questions.text: On which factor does the boiling point of a liquid not depend?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The boiling point is directly proportional to the external pressure The boiling point of water at sea level=100oC The boiling point of water on Mount everest=68oC
The boiling point is inversely proportional to vapor pressure.
The boiling point is independent of the amount of liquid present. 1L of water are 100ml of water have the same boiling point.
The boiling point is directly proportional to IMF i.e. BP of water>ethylalcohol.

questions.explanation: The boiling point of a liquid doesn’t depend on the amount of liquid or the surface area of a liquid. It does depend on the intermolecular forces, vapor pressure, and external pressure. The intermolecular forces determine the amount of energy needed to overcome bonds. When a liquid is heated, the vapor pressure increases. At one point, the vapor pressure reaches the external pressure. This is the boiling point.

questions.text: Which of the substance has the highest melting point?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Alkyl halides, also known as haloalkanes, are organic compounds that contain a halogen atom bonded to an alkyl group. They are not structurally related to carboxylic acids and do not contain a carboxylic acid functional group.

H2O has a melting point of 0 °C because it has hydrogen bonds between its water molecules. In the case of water, the hydrogen atoms of one molecule are attracted to the oxygen atoms of another molecule, forming hydrogen bonds.

NaCl has a high melting point because it has ionic bonds between its sodium and chloride ions. In the case of NaCl, the positively charged sodium ions and negatively charged chloride ions are held together by these ionic bonds, which require a large amount of energy to break. This results in a high melting point of 801 °C for NaCl.

Magnesium oxide has ionic bonds between its magnesium and oxygen atoms, which are very strong electrostatic forces of attraction between oppositely charged ions. This results in a very high melting point for MgO, which is 2852 °C.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct since in Magnesium Oxide, both Magnesium and Oxygen atoms form ions in the lattice. Due to the higher magnitude of the charge on the Magnesium (+2) and Oxygen atoms (-2), the force of attraction between them is very high. This provides them with very high lattice energy and a lot of energy is required to break this lattice; hence the melting point of MgO is high. MgO - 2852o CH2O - 100 oCCO2- 78.46 oCNaCl - 801 oC

The critical temperature is the highest temperature at which it is possible to separate substances into two fluid phases. According to experiments, ammonia has a critical temperature of 132 °C. Hence, option C is the correct option.
This value is incorrect. It is not the critical temperature of ammonia.

questions.explanation: The critical temperature is the highest temperature at which it is possible to separate substances into two fluid phases. This is 132 °C for ammonia.

questions.text: Vapor pressure is measured by calculating the difference in liquid pressure and?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Mercury pressure is incorrect because it does not have any direct relation to the measurement of vapor pressure.
Glass pressure is incorrect because it does not have any direct relation to the measurement of vapor pressure.

Vapor pressure or equilibrium vapor pressure is defined as the pressure exerted by a vapor in thermodynamic equilibrium with its condensed phases at a given temperature in a closed system. The equilibrium vapor pressure is an indication of a liquid's evaporation rate.

Container pressure is also incorrect because the pressure exerted by the vapor in the container is a part of the container pressure itself. To obtain the vapor pressure, we need to subtract the atmospheric pressure from the total pressure exerted by the mixture of vapor and air in the container.

questions.explanation: Option A) Mercury pressure and Option B) Glass pressure are incorrect because they do not have any direct relation to the measurement of vapor pressure.Option C) Vapor pressure or equilibrium vapor pressure is defined as the pressure exerted by a vapor in thermodynamic equilibrium with its condensed phases at a given temperature in a closed system. The equilibrium vapor pressure is an indication of a liquid's evaporation rate.Option D) Container pressure is also incorrect because the pressure exerted by the vapor in the container is a part of the container pressure itself. To obtain the vapor pressure, we need to subtract the atmospheric pressure from the total pressure exerted by the mixture of vapor and air in the container.

questions.text: Boiling needs _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: Supply of heat in the start
options.explanation: a) Supply of heat in the startExplanation: Boiling starts when a liquid is heated, and the temperature of the liquid reaches its boiling point. At the boiling point, the liquid changes phase into a gas, forming bubbles throughout the liquid.

Boiling needs a continuous and constant heat supply. As a liquid is heated, the kinetic energy of its molecules increases, and hence the temperature also increases. At the boiling point, the kinetic energy of the molecules becomes maximum and any further heating at this stage will not increase the temperature. If the heat supply cuts before the boiling point is reached, the kinetic energy of the liquid molecules starts to decrease.

options.text: Supply of heat at the end
options.explanation: c) Supply of heat at the endExplanation: This option is not correct. Boiling does not require heat to be supplied at the end. Boiling is a continuous process that occurs when the liquid is heated to its boiling point.

d) Supply of heat variablyExplanation: This option is not correct. Boiling requires a constant supply of heat, not a variable supply. The heat is needed to maintain the boiling process and convert the liquid into a gas.

questions.explanation: Boiling needs a continuous and constant heat supply. As a liquid is heated, the kinetic energy of its molecules increases, and hence the temperature also increases. At the boiling point, the kinetic energy of the molecules becomes maximum and any further heating at this stage will not increase the temperature. If the heat supply cuts before the boiling point is reached, the kinetic energy of the liquid molecules starts to decrease.

questions.text: A jar contains a gas and a few drops of water at 'T' Kelvin temperature. The pressure in the jar is 830 mmHg. The temperature of the jar is reduced by 1%. The vapour pressure of water at two temperatures is 30 and 25 mm of Hg, respectively. Calculate the new pressure in the jar?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The pressure of the dry gas changes with temperature according to the ideal gas law, while the vapor pressure of water changes to 25 mm of Hg. The total pressure becomes 792 + 25 = 817 mm of Hg.
This is the pressure of the dry gas only, without considering the new vapor pressure of water at the reduced temperature.
This is the initial total pressure in the jar before the temperature change, and doesn't account for the decreased temperature and vapor pressure.
This value does not correspond to any logical calculation based on the problem's given data and conditions.

questions.explanation: This is the following solution:Pressure in the jar =830 mm of Hg.Pressure of moisture =30 mm of Hg.Pressure of dry gas =830−30=800 mm of Hg.Since the volume is constant and the temperature is changing, using the ideal gas equation,​T1/P1 ​​=​T2/P2 ​​⇒ T1/800 ​​=​0.99T1/P2 ​​⇒P2​=0.99×800=792 mm of Hg.Therefore, the total pressure =792+25=817 mm of Hg. questions.text: Which of the following exerts the lowest vapour pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Chloroform has a lower vapor pressure compared to the other options. It has relatively strong intermolecular forces, so it doesn't easily evaporate at room temperature.
Methanol has a higher vapor pressure compared to chloroform. It has weaker intermolecular forces, allowing it to evaporate more readily
Water has a lower vapor pressure compared to methanol and chloroform. It has relatively strong intermolecular forces, which make it less likely to evaporate quickly.
Ethanol has a higher vapor pressure compared to water but lower than methanol. It has weaker intermolecular forces, allowing it to evaporate more easily than water.

questions.explanation: The stronger the intermolecular forces, the lower the vapor pressure. Water has the strongest forces amongst these options (4 H-bonds), hence it will have the lowest vapor pressure.

questions.text: What is the relationship between boiling point and External pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: Boiling point increases if external pressure is increased
options.explanation: The boiling point is the temperature when the internal/vapor pressure of the liquid becomes equal to the external/atmospheric pressure. If external pressure increases, the vapor pressure must increase as well and become equal to the external pressure for the liquid to boil. As temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point increases if external pressure decreases
options.explanation: It is incorrect because as temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point is not affected by external pressure
options.explanation: It is incorrect because as temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point decreases if external pressure increased
options.explanation: It is incorrect because as temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

If external pressure increases, the vapor pressure must increase as well and become equal to the external pressure for the liquid to boil. As temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

questions.text: The highest boiling point among the following is for:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ammonia has hydrogen bonding due to the presence of a nitrogen atom bonded to hydrogen, which gives it a relatively high boiling point compared to nonpolar molecules. However, it is lower than the boiling points of more complex molecules with stronger intermolecular forces.

Bromine is a diatomic nonpolar molecule that experiences London dispersion forces. While it has a higher boiling point than many small nonpolar molecules, it is still lower than that of polar molecules with stronger intermolecular forces.

Chloroform is a polar molecule and exhibits dipole-dipole interactions along with London dispersion forces. Its boiling point is elevated due to these stronger intermolecular forces compared to the other options listed.

Xenon is a noble gas and is nonpolar, primarily experiencing London dispersion forces. Although it has a relatively high boiling point for noble gases, it is lower than that of chloroform, which has stronger intermolecular forces.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is CHCl3 (Chloroform) because it has significant dipole-dipole interactions due to its polar nature, which elevates its boiling point compared to the other options. NH3 (Ammonia) has hydrogen bonding, but it has a lower boiling point than chloroform. Br2 (Bromine), being a diatomic nonpolar molecule, has weaker London dispersion forces, resulting in a lower boiling point. Lastly, Xe (Xenon) is a noble gas, and while it possesses London dispersion forces, it lacks the strong polar interactions found in chloroform, leading to an even lower boiling point.

questions.text: Upon which factor is vapor pressure not dependent:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The greater the temperature, the more molecules will gain sufficient K.E and hence, the greater the vapor pressure. Thus, the V.P of a liquid is dependent on the temperature.

The stronger the intermolecular forces, the lesser will be the V.P as the molecules will be tightly bound to each other and won’t be able to escape easily. Hence, the V.P is also inversely dependent on the intermolecular forces.
As the density increases the pressure also increases due to a decrease in the volume of the liquid. Hence, V.P is also dependent on the density of the liquid.

The V.P. of a liquid is independent of the surface area of the liquid. This is because the rate of evaporation is balanced with the rate of condensation. And any increase in the surface area will not be sufficient to increase the V.P until the molecules don’t have sufficient energy of action. Hence, this is the correct option.

questions.explanation: The surface area of liquid or solid in contact does not affect vapor pressure. Other factors do have an impact on vapor pressure. Higher density decreases vapor pressure, higher temperature increases vapor pressure and higher intermolecular forces also decrease the vapor pressure.

questions.text: Independent molecule of NaCl may exist in:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: In the solid phase, NaCl exists as a crystalline lattice with strong ionic bonds between sodium and chloride ions, not as independent molecules.
In the molten phase, NaCl ions are free to move but still exist in an ionic form, not as independent molecules.
NaCl does not exist as an independent molecule in the liquid phase. It remains in an ionic state.
In the vapour phase, NaCl can exist as independent molecules because the ionic bonds are overcome by heat, allowing the molecules to separate and exist independently.

questions.explanation: Sodium chloride (NaCl) is an ionic compound that forms a giant ionic lattice in solid and liquid states.In the solid or aqueous state, NaCl does not exist as independent molecules; instead, it exists as ions (Na⁺ and Cl⁻) arranged in a lattice or dissolved in solution.However, when NaCl is vaporized at very high temperatures, individual NaCl molecules can exist temporarily in the gas phase.

CH4 (methane) has only London dispersion forces, which are the weakest intermolecular forces. As a result, CH4 has the lowest boiling point among the given options.

questions.text: Correct order of boiling points of the given liquids is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This order is not correct when considering boiling points.
This order is not correct when considering boiling points.

The boiling point of a liquid is directly related to the strength of intermolecular forces between its molecules. The strongest intermolecular force is hydrogen bonding, followed by dipole-dipole interactions, and then London dispersion forces.Water (H2O) has the highest boiling point because it can form a maximum number of hydrogen bonds.Hydrogen fluoride (HF) has the second-highest boiling point because it can form hydrogen bonds as well, but to a lesser extent than H2O.Ammonia (NH3) has weaker hydrogen bonding between its molecules than water and HF, but still stronger than hydrogen chloride (HCl), making it the liquid with the third-highest boiling point.Hydrogen chloride (HCl) has very weak hydrogen bonding or no hydrogen bonding at all between its molecules, making it the liquid with the lowest boiling point among the given options.Therefore, the correct order of boiling points is H2O > HF > NH3 > HCl.
This order is not correct when considering boiling points.

questions.explanation: H2O has a greater boiling point than HF because H2O can form 4 hydrogen bonds while HF has 2 hydrogen bonds and to break all the hydrogen bonds more energy is needed by H2O than HF. Due to the strong force of intermolecular hydrogen bonding. Due to the high difference in the electronegativity of hydrogen and fluorine as compared to that of hydrogen and nitrogen, more amount of energy is required to break the hydrogen-fluorine bond. Because of the high electronegativity of nitrogen than chlorine, the bond between N−H is stronger than a bond between H−Cl. Therefore the boiling point of NH3 is more than HCl.

questions.text: The densities of liquids are much _ than gases but _ to solids.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is wrong because the density of the liquid is not smaller than gas.

Density: The molecules of a liquid are packed relatively close together. Consequently, liquids are much denser than gases. The density of a liquid is typically about the same as the density of the solid state of the substance. In general, solids are denser than liquids, which are denser than gases. The particles in the solid are touching with very little space between them. The particles in a liquid usually are still touching but there are some spaces between them. The gas particles have big distances between them. Thus, liquids and gases are not very dense. On the other hand, the particles in solids are present very close to each other and the intermolecular gaps are very small. The intermolecular forces are thus high giving them making them rigid and dense. Thus, solids are denser than liquids and gases. The density of solids is always greater than liquids and gases.
This option is wrong because the density of liquids is not smaller than gases and not far away to solids.
This option is wrong because the density of the liquid is not far away from solids.

questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because the density of the liquid is not smaller than gas. Option: B Density: The molecules of a liquid are packed relatively close together. Consequently, liquids are much denser than gases. The particles in a liquid usually are still touching but there are some spaces between them. The gas particles have big distances between them. Thus, liquids and gases are not very dense. ✓ On the other hand, the particles in solids are present very close to each other and the intermolecular gaps are very small. The intermolecular forces are thus high giving them making them rigid and dense. Thus, solids are denser than liquids and gases. ✓ The density of solids is always greater than liquids and gases. Option C is wrong because the density of liquids is not smaller than gases and not far away from solids. Option D is wrong because the density of the liquid is not far away from solids.

questions.text: Which forces are very significant in non-polar molecules like Cl2, H2and noble gases?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This is incorrect as dipole-dipole forces occur between polar molecules, not non-polar molecules.
Partially correct, but not the primary force. Induced dipole interactions are part of London dispersion forces which are the main forces in non-polar substances.
This is not the correct choice as it involves interactions between a polar molecule and a non-polar molecule, not typical in non-polar-only substances.
Correct! London dispersion forces, also known as spontaneous-induced dipole interactions, are significant in non-polar molecules, contributing to their intermolecular attractions.

questions.explanation: In non-polar molecules such as Cl2, H2, and noble gases, the predominant intermolecular forces are London dispersion forces. These forces arise from temporary fluctuations in electron density, which induce a temporary dipole that can affect neighboring molecules. This cumulative effect leads to attractive forces even in the absence of permanent dipoles. Other options such as dipole-dipole and dipole-induced dipole interactions require the presence of polar molecules, which are not relevant here.

questions.text: With the increasing molecular mass of hydrocarbons, they change from
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: For smaller hydrocarbons with lower molecular masses, the predominant intermolecular forces are typically weak van der Waals forces or London dispersion forces. As the molecular mass increases, these forces become stronger, causing the hydrocarbons to transition from a gaseous state to a liquid state at a given temperature.

For larger hydrocarbons with higher molecular masses, the increase in molecular size and mass leads to stronger intermolecular forces. In some cases, these forces become significant enough to hold the molecules in a more ordered, solid arrangement at a given temperature, resulting in the transition from a liquid to a solid state.

London forces are directly related to the mass of the molecules. So the attractive force between higher hydrocarbons is more. This is why the state of hydrocarbons is changing from gas --> liquid --> solid on increasing the number of carbon atoms.
This option is incorrect because, as explained, the trend for hydrocarbons with increasing molecular mass is often a transition from gases to liquids, and in some cases, to solids.

questions.explanation: London forces are directly related to the mass of the molecules. So the attractive force between higher hydrocarbons is more. This is why the state of hydrocarbons is changing from gas --> liquid --> solid on increasing the number of carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since the size of all electrons is the same.

Option B is correct since the London dispersion force is a temporary attractive force that results when the electrons in two adjacent atoms occupy positions that make the atoms form temporary dipoles. This force is sometimes called an induced dipole-induced dipole attraction. questions.text: Due to which of the following forces present, is aquatic life protected in cold climate?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Dipole-dipole forces do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

Debye forces, also known as London dispersion forces, do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

Hydrogen bonding is a type of intermolecular force that occurs between a hydrogen atom bonded to an electronegative atom (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom in a different molecule. In the case of antifreeze proteins, these proteins contain regions that are rich in amino acids that can form hydrogen bonds with ice crystals. When the protein binds to the surface of an ice crystal, it prevents further growth of the crystal by inhibiting the addition of more water molecules to the crystal lattice. This allows the organism to survive in sub-zero temperatures by preventing ice from forming inside its cells and causing damage to its tissues.

London dispersion forces, also known as Van der Waals forces, do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is a type of intermolecular force that occurs between a hydrogen atom bonded to an electronegative atom (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom in a different molecule. In the case of antifreeze proteins, these proteins contain regions that are rich in amino acids that can form hydrogen bonds with ice crystals. When the protein binds to the surface of an ice crystal, it prevents further growth of the crystal by inhibiting the addition of more water molecules to the crystal lattice. This allows the organism to survive in sub-zero temperatures by preventing ice from forming inside its cells and causing damage to its tissues.
hints: aquatic life protected in cold climate

questions.text: Which of the following has least polarizability?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: NH3 (ammonia) has polarizability. While ammonia is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

H2O (water) has polarizability. Water is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, but it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

HF (hydrogen fluoride) has polarizability. While HF is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

Methane (CH4) has the least polarizability of these molecules because it is a nonpolar molecule. This means that it has no permanent dipole moment and its electron density is distributed evenly around the molecule. Since polarizability is a measure of how easily the electron cloud of a molecule can be distorted by an electric field, nonpolar molecules like methane have very low polarizabilities. In contrast, polar molecules with large dipole moments have higher polarizabilities because their electron clouds are more easily distorted by external electric fields.

questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) has the least polarizability of these molecules because it is a nonpolar molecule. This means that it has no permanent dipole moment and its electron density is distributed evenly around the molecule. Since polarizability is a measure of how easily the electron cloud of a molecule can be distorted by an electric field, nonpolar molecules like methane have very low polarizabilities. In contrast, polar molecules with large dipole moments have higher polarizabilities because their electron clouds are more easily distorted by external electric fields.
